claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria ColorArea and ColorSlider native range inputs, ColorSwatchPicker option collection, group labelling, and composed ColorField text input
semantics.naming: The picker, 2D area, each HSB and alpha channel, swatch collection, swatch option, and exact text editor have localized visible or programmatic names.
semantics.roleOverride: No consumer role overrides are supported. Mergora does not synthesize a custom two-axis role; React Aria exposes the area through native range inputs and a documented 2D slider roledescription.
semantics.refTarget: HTMLDivElement root; ColorField inputRef reaches the exact text editor

interaction.keyboard: The area supports React Aria two-axis keys. Named hue, saturation, brightness, and optional alpha native range inputs provide complete independent keyboard alternatives; swatches use single-selection collection keys and the text editor supports exact entry.
interaction.pointer: Primary pointer interaction on the 2D area, channel tracks, and swatches updates the same controlled value with React Aria cancellation behavior.
interaction.touch: Area and tracks use touch-action management from React Aria. Coarse-pointer thumbs, tracks, swatches, and the editor target at least 44 CSS pixels.
interaction.speech: Visible picker, channel, swatch, and field labels remain available for voice targeting; canonical values are visible text.
interaction.switchAccess: Every dimension has a native range or text input path and presets are real collection options; no pointer-only task remains.

formBehavior: The embedded ColorField owns one optional canonical hidden successful control and native reset. Area, channel, and swatch controls never add duplicate successful values. Disabled values are omitted; read-only values remain successful.

states.synchronization: All spatial, channel, preset, text, preview, contrast, and form surfaces derive from one immutable 8-bit sRGB value and controlled owner.

preferences.dark: Uses semantic surface, border, text, and focus tokens while color values remain explicit samples.
preferences.enhancedContrast: Selected swatches add border geometry, weight, a check, and canonical text; channel outputs remain visible.
preferences.forcedColors: Authored gradients and fills are replaced with system Canvas, CanvasText, Highlight, borders, outlines, checks, labels, outputs, and canonical text without opting out for the whole component.
preferences.reducedMotion: No essential information or operation depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: The area changes aspect ratio and channel/swatch grids become one column in narrow containers; labels, outputs, and canonical values wrap without page-level overflow.

internationalization.imePolicy: The composed text editor owns composition-safe validation; sliders and swatches do not intercept text composition.
internationalization.serialization: Channel names and numbers follow the React Aria provider locale. CSS text and the canonical #rrggbb or #rrggbbaa form value remain ASCII machine data under bidi isolation.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a persistent visible Field label and localize picker, field, and swatch labels.
Choose alphaPolicy and presets that match the domain; opaque mode rejects translucent values rather than silently flattening them.
Use the text and channel alternatives in documentation instead of teaching a pointer-only 2D workflow.
Pass the real opaque contrast background and verify final computed colors, text size, states, layering, themes, and consumer overrides.
limitations: The picker intentionally exposes 8-bit sRGB through HSB spatial/channel controls and exact hex/RGB/HSL text. It does not claim wide-gamut, perceptual-space, eyedropper, canvas sampling, or arbitrary CSS color-function support.
The 2D area is not the sole path: named sliders and the text editor are required parts of the component and documentation contract.
Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
